title: -ա

etymology.text: Abstracted away from European female given names commonly ending in -''a''.

definition: neologism, rare Forming fanciful female given names.

title: -ա-
etymology: Armenian suffix - see etymology of related words

title: -աբար
-ագին
-ագույն
-ալ

etymology.text: From Old Armenian infinitive suffix -ալ. From PIE *-l- suffix forming verbal nouns.

definition: One of two suffixes added to verb stems to form their infinitive, the other being -ել.
definition_en: One of two suffixes added to verb stems to form their infinitive, the other being -el.
etymology_en: From Old Armenian infinitive suffix -al. From PIE *-l- suffix forming verbal nouns.

title: -ած
definition: Appended to the verb stem to form its resultative participle.

title: -ածո
definition: Forms nouns and adjectives with the meaning of a consequence of an action.

title: -ածու

etymology.text: From Old Armenian -ածու. Forms nouns from past participles.

etymology_en: From Old Armenian - atsu. Forms nouns from past participles.

title: -ակ

etymology.text: Borrowed via Classical Armenian from Middle Iranian *-ak. Compare Old Georgian -აკი. Ultimately from Proto-Iranian *-akah.

title: -ական

-անց

etymology.text: From Old Armenian -անց. Locative suffix meaning 'through, via'.

etymology_en: From Old Armenian Locative suffix meaning 'through, via'.

title: -անք

etymology.text: Martirosyan (2011), p. 366: Step`anos Orbelean (1250/60-1304) mentions J/lmidtildeahayreank`*, of which no etymological explanation is known to me. It seems to reflect the above-mentioned Me/lmidtilderi form: *jə/lmidtildeahari + -an-k`.  ETYM A/lmidtildeayan (1954: 278b) restores *jo/lmidtildehari not specifying the structure and the origin. As is implicitly suggested by H. Muradyan (see above), the compound seems to contain jo/lmidtilde `log; pole' (> Me/lmidtilderi ju/lmidtildeϵ). The second component is har- `to beat, strike', represented in another compound, viz. Me/lmidtilderi *tirivharϵ [A/lmidtildeayan 1954: 332], Kak`avaberd tirivhari `a sharp instrument for cutting off leaves and/or branches of.
etymology.source: Martirosyan, H. (2011), Studies in Armenian Etymology

title: -ապան
-ապանակ
-ապատ
definition: forming city names after the name of its founder.

title: -ապես
-աստան
-ավետ
-ավոր
definition: Forming names of persons or things, carrying or doing something.

title: -ավուն

etymology.text: The origin is. Confined to Eastern dialects, with the earliest attestation in the 17th century.<ref></ref> Ačaṙyan remarks that derivation from (of Iranian origin) is impossible, because no → sound change is known in the dialects. But perhaps that change occurred within an language, where such a change is known, and thence was borrowed into Armenian. Compare also , forming adjectives from nouns.

title: -ատ
-արան
definition: forming nouns of places or containers; -ery, -arium.

title: -արար
-արեն
definition_en: (Suffix) Forms adverbs, corresponding to the English "-ly".

title: -արք

etymology.text: From Old Armenian -արք. Instrumental suffix forming nouns.

etymology_en: From Old Armenian -arch. Instrumental suffix forming nouns.

title: -ացի

etymology.text: From Old Armenian -ացի. Denominal suffix forming demonyms.

etymology_en: From Old Armenian - atsi. Denominal suffix forming demonyms.

title: -ացն-
wikitext:
==Armenian==

===Etymology===
From {{uder|hy|xcl|-ուցան-|}}.

===Pronunciation===
{{hy-IPA|w=ացըն}}

===Suffix===
{{head|hy|suffix|cat2=inflectional suffixes}}

# Infixed into suffixed verbs ending with {{m|hy|-ալ}} in infinitive to create their [[causative]] version. The verb suffix is replaced by the infix and the infinitive ending changes to {{m|hy|-ել}}.
#: {{l|hy|հեռանալ}} — ''to go away''
#:: {{l|hy|հեռացնել}} — ''to remove, drive away''
#: {{l|hy|ուրախանալ}} — ''to be happy''
#:: {{l|hy|ուրախացնել}} — ''to make happy''

====See also====
* {{l|hy|-ցն-}}
* {{l|hy|-եցն-}}
definition: Infixed into suffixed verbs ending with -ալ in infinitive to create their causative version. The verb suffix is replaced by the infix and the infinitive ending changes to -ել.
definition_en: Infixed into suffixed verbs ending with -ал in infinitive to create their causative version. The verb suffix is replaced by the infix and the infinitive ending changes to -el.

title: -ացու
-բան

etymology.text: From. In Old Armenian it was used in Ancient Greek [[calque]]s for translating in words like or , which were rendered as and. The practice continues now in calquing international (Latin) words with , , etc.

title: -դ
definition: a possessive suffix marking 2nd person possession.

title: -ե

etymology.text: Inherited form of . The learned form is .

definition: forms adjectives with the meaning of “made or consisting of”; -en.

title: -ել
definition: One of two suffixes added to verb stems to form their infinitive, the other being -ալ.
definition_en: One of two suffixes added to verb stems to form their infinitive, the other being -al.

-եղեն
-են-

title: -ենի

definition: forming names of trees and shrubs.

title: -եր

etymology.text: From , which was used for forming collective nouns, e.g. , , etc.

definition: plural nominative case marker for monosyllabic words.

title: -երեն
definition: Forms names of languages when appended to roots denoting names of nations or regions:.

title: -երորդ
-եցի

etymology.text: From Old Armenian -եցի. From PIE *-ik- + *-yo-. Forms demonyms.

etymology_en: From Old Armenian -etsi. From PIE *-ik- + *-yo-. Forms demonyms.

title: -եցն-
wikitext:
==Armenian==

===Etymology===
From {{uder|hy|xcl|-ուցան-}}.

===Pronunciation===
{{hy-IPA|w=էցըն}}

===Suffix===
{{head|hy|suffix|cat2=inflectional suffixes}}

# Infixed into verbs ending with {{m|hy|-ել}} in infinitive to create their [[causative]] version.
#: {{l|hy|քնել}} — ''to sleep''
#:: {{l|hy|քնեցնել}} — ''to put to sleep''
#: {{l|hy|պայթել}} — ''(intransitive) to explode''
#:: {{l|hy|պայթեցնել}} — ''(transitive) to blow up, detonate''

====See also====
{{col|hy|-ացն-|-ցն-}}
definition: Infixed into verbs ending with -ել in infinitive to create their causative version.
definition_en: Infixed into verbs ending with -ел in infinitive to create their causative version.

title: -ը
definition: a definite article that assigns definite reference to the noun and is suffixed to the noun’s stem, or to the plural suffix or to the case ending.

title: -ի
-իլ

etymology.text: From the post-classical Old Armenian infinitive ending -իլ, which was created following the mediopassive present tense verb endings.

definition: One of four suffixes added to verb stems to form their infinitive, the others being -ել, -ալ, and -ուլ.
definition_en: One of four suffixes added to verb stems to form their infinitive, the others being -ел, -ал, and -ул.
etymology_en: From the post-classical Old Armenian infinitive ending -il, which was created following the mediopassive present tense verb endings.

title: -իլիոն
-իկ
definition: forming diminutives or terms expressing affection.

title: -իչ
-իս

etymology.text: From Old Armenian -իս. Locative case ending, fossilized.

definition: Appended to the verb to form its second present participle.
etymology_en: From Old Armenian. Locative case ending, fossilized.

title: -ից
definition: Eastern Armenian the singular and plural ablative case ending of nouns of almost all declension types.

title: -իք
-լիկ

etymology.text: From Old Armenian -լիկ. Diminutive suffix from PIE *-liko-.

definition: Used to form adjectives, to denote possession or presence of a quality.
etymology_en: From Old Armenian - lik. Diminutive suffix from PIE *-liko-.

title: -կերտ
definition: forming city names after the name of its founder.

title: -կոտ

etymology.text: From Turkish -ci (occupational suffix) via Ottoman. Forms agent nouns.
